title: 03617_Data_Feedback_Loops_Model-driven_Amplification_of_Dataset_Biases
positive_examples: Proposes an intervention to help mitigate and stabilize unstable feedback systems in machine learning models.
Demonstrates that models exhibiting sampling-like behavior are more stable and faithful, potentially leading to better model performance.
negative_examples: Data feedback loops can selectively amplify certain biases, making it harder to tune models to be unbiased.
The utility of the internet as a data source may decrease rapidly as model outputs replace human annotations, leading to a degradation of data quality.
Bias amplification in model predictions can exacerbate existing societal biases, such as gender bias in visual role-labeling tasks.
